Example data for preprocessing individual-level genotype inputs

Data_preprocessI.Rd

A small example dataset used to demonstrate genotype preprocessing with preprocessI before fitting BRIERi. The example is based on a height PRS prediction analysis in the Michigan Genomics Initiative (MGI), where the target cohort contains African-ancestry individuals and the external models are derived from European-ancestry resources.

Format

A list with three elements:

target.info

A data.frame with 10,000 rows describing the SNPs in the target genotype matrix. Each row corresponds to one target genotype column. The data.frame includes columns CHR (chromosome), BP (base-pair position), A2 (reference allele), and A1 (alternative/effect allele), following PLINK conventions where A1 is the effect allele.

external.coef1

A data.frame with 10,000 rows containing SNP-level coefficients from the first external model. The required columns are CHR, BP, REF, ALT, and coef, where coef is the external model coefficient for the corresponding SNP.

external.coef2

A data.frame with 163,140 rows containing SNP-level coefficients from the second external model. The required columns are CHR, BP, REF, ALT, and coef. This external model has substantially more SNPs than external.coef1, allowing users to demonstrate merging and alignment of multiple external models.

Source

Michigan Genomics Initiative (MGI), African-ancestry subcohort (target.info); MGI European-ancestry subcohort (external.coef1); published European-ancestry height GWAS summary statistics (external.coef2). Raw individual-level data are not redistributed.

Details

The dataset contains SNP annotation information for the target genotype matrix and two external SNP-level coefficient files. Raw individual-level genotype and phenotype data are not included due to privacy constraints.

This object is intended for illustrating the preprocessing workflow: first merge multiple external coefficient files with mergeExternals, then align the merged external coefficients to the target SNP set using preprocessI. The output of preprocessI can be used to subset the target genotype matrix and construct the beta.external matrix required by BRIERi.

Because BRIERi expects an intercept slot as the first row of beta.external, users should prepend a zero row to the aligned external coefficient matrix when no external intercept is used.

See also

preprocessI, mergeExternals, BRIERi

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
data(Data_preprocessI)
names(Data_preprocessI)
} # }
